blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Renal Development?
Kidney เป็น organ ที่ sensitive ต่อ prenatal insults มากที่สุดอวัยวะหนึ่ง — nephrogenesis สิ้นสุดที่ 34–36 สัปดาห์: ทารกที่คลอดก่อนกำหนดทุกรายเสี่ยง nephron deficit ตลอดชีวิต. 6 ข้อสำคัญ: (1) Nephron number = 200,000–2,000,000 ต่อไต 1 ข้าง; >60% ถูกสร้างใน trimester 3; (2) Preterm birth → nephrogenesis หยุดก่อนกำหนด → CKD + HTN ในผู้ใหญ่; (3) RET–GDNF pathway คือ master switch ของ ureteral bud — ผิดปกติ → CAKUT; (4) WT1 transcription factor สำคัญต่อ podocyte → WAGR syndrome + Denys-Drash syndrome; (5) Brenner hypothesis: low nephron number → hyperfiltration → HTN → CKD; (6) Antenatal glucocorticoids (แม้ 1 course) → ลด GFR + ↑ insulin resistance ในผู้ใหญ่.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย 3 kidney systems ตามลำดับพัฒนาการ: pronephros, mesonephros, metanephros และ timeline การ differentiation
อธิบาย pathway RET–GDNF–Wnt11 ใน ureteral bud formation และ branching — เชื่อมกับ CAKUT pathogenesis
อธิบาย nephron development stages: renal vesicle → comma-shaped body → S-shaped body → mature nephron
รู้ role ของ VEGF ใน glomerular vasculogenesis + podocyte foot process ใน filtration barrier
รู้ role ของ WT1 ใน podocyte development; เชื่อม WAGR syndrome + Denys-Drash syndrome
อธิบาย RAS interaction ใน fetal renal development: AT1 vs AT2 receptors, sex-specific programming
อธิบาย renal ascent และ mechanism การ migrate จาก sacral → L1 vertebra
อธิบาย Brenner hypothesis และ evidence เชื่อม LBW + preterm birth กับ CKD + HTN ในผู้ใหญ่
รู้ environmental factors ที่ลด nephron number: prematurity, LBW, maternal drugs, NSAID, glucocorticoids
อธิบาย epigenetic mechanisms ที่ programming renal function ตั้งแต่ fetal life
1. Three Kidney Systems — Timeline พัฒนาการ
1.1 Pronephros, Mesonephros, Metanephros

rows: Partially persists: ♂ → vas deferens, epididymis; ♀ → degenerates
Permanent kidney — filtration, homeostasis, hormone production
Becomes adult kidney; nephrogenesis complete 34–36 wks

blocks.text: Ureteric branching ceases ~week 14–15 of gestation. Human kidney = multipapillate with 8–15 lobes, each with ~15 generations of collecting duct branching.
1.2 Nephron Number — สำคัญแค่ไหน
Normal range: 200,000–2,000,000 nephrons/kidney; peak growth ใน third trimester
Nephrogenesis complete 34–36 weeks; >60% ของ nephrons ถูกสร้างใน T3 (especially 28–34 wks)
After birth: kidney growth = nephron hypertrophy only — nephron number ไม่เพิ่มอีก
Females มี glomeruli น้อยกว่า males ~12% โดยเฉลี่ย แต่ CKD risk similar

⚡ Key Number: Nephron deficit เริ่มที่ GA < 36 wks
ทารกที่คลอดก่อน 36 wks ยังอยู่ใน active nephrogenesis — การคลอดทำให้ nephrogenesis หยุด ณ จุดนั้น. ยิ่ง premature มากยิ่ง nephron น้อย. Nephron number ที่ได้เป็น ตัวกำหนด lifetime renal reserve — ไม่มีวิธีเพิ่มจำนวน nephron หลังคลอด.
2. Renal Morphogenesis — Molecular Mechanisms
2.1 RET–GDNF Pathway: Master Switch ของ Ureteral Bud
GDNF (glial cell-derived neurotrophic factor): located in metanephric mesenchyme; activates RET (receptor tyrosine kinase) on nephric duct → ureteral bud evagination
RET absent → ureteric induction fails completely; GDNF placed ectopically → ectopic ureteral bud → ureteral ectopia, VUR, or UPJ obstruction
Ureteral bud branching = tips only; RET–Wnt11 loop upregulates GDNF secretion → continued branching; Wnt path factors + FGFs further upregulate GDNF
PAX2 + PAX8: necessary for nephric duct development, elongation, maintenance

Apoptosis of common nephric duct (downregulation of RET) → separates ureter from nephric duct → forms ureterovesical junction; delayed apoptosis → UPJ obstruction; overactivation → vesicoureteral reflux
2.2 Nephron Development Stages

rows: First epithelial structure; polarized epithelial state; mesenchyme → epithelium transition
Activated by ureteral bud + Wnt genes
First condensation of renal vesicle mesenchyme; cleft begins to form
Cleft deepens; proximal end = glomerulus; distal end = collecting duct
VEGF from podocytes → recruit endothelial progenitors → glomerular vasculogenesis
Endothelial infiltration into glomerular cleft; podocyte foot processes form
Glomerulus + proximal tubule + loop of Henle + distal tubule + collecting duct
All connect to ureteric tree collecting duct

blocks.text:
S-shaped Body = จุดเชื่อม Nephrogenesis กับ Vasculogenesis
ที่ S-shaped body stage: podocytes หลั่ง VEGF → endothelial progenitors migrate เข้า cleft → glomerular tuft. นี่คือจุดที่ nephron development + vascular development เชื่อมกันครั้งแรก. Failure ของ VEGF-podocyte signaling ใน stage นี้ = malformed glomerulus.
3. Glomerular Development — Podocytes, VEGF, WT1
3.1 Podocyte Foot Processes และ Filtration Barrier
Podocytes อยู่ที่ outer surface ของ glomerular capillary; foot processes interdigitate → filtration slits → selective barrier: เปิดให้ water + small solutes ผ่าน แต่กั้น proteins
Loss of foot process integrity → proteinuria — hallmark ของ glomerular disease

Podocytes หลั่ง VEGF-A: (1) fenestration ของ endothelial cells; (2) promotes capillary development ใน glomerular tuft (initial 1 capillary loop → mature 6–8 loops); (3) regulates glomerular basement membrane assembly
3.2 WT1 (Wilms Tumor 1) — Transcription Factor
WT1: zinc finger transcription factor, chr 11p13; 4 isoforms; acts as transcriptional activator OR repressor depending on organ/stage
Essential for podocyte development and maintenance; also expressed in metanephric mesenchyme driving GDNF expression → ureteral bud outgrowth

rows: Wilms' tumor, Aniridia, GU abnormalities, mental Retardation
Mesangial sclerosis + Wilms' tumor + gonadal dysgenesis; severe early-onset nephrotic syndrome
Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis + gonadal dysgenesis; no Wilms' tumor

blocks.text: 4. Ureteral Growth and Development
4.1 Smooth Muscle Differentiation
Ureteral smooth muscle differentiation: เริ่มที่ distal ureter (UVJ) → ascend proximally to UPJ (embryonic day E13.5–E16.5 in animal model)
Peristaltic waves: pacemaker cells ใน renal pelvis → spontaneous electrical oscillations → proximal-to-distal wave propagation → independent of neural input
BMP4 (bone morphogenetic protein 4): signals for distal ureteral development; ↓BMP4 → ectopic ureteral bud + ectopic ureters
Sonic hedgehog (Shh): expressed by ureteral epithelium → stimulates mesenchymal growth of ureter; BMP4 stimulates Shh to differentiate smooth muscle cells
4.2 Urothelium
Urothelium = specialized stratified epithelium lining renal pelvis → proximal urethra; mesodermal origin (vs bladder epithelium = endodermal)
Uroplakin proteins ใน urothelial plaques → impermeable barrier to urine
Abnormal distal ureteral maturation → ureterocele (blind ureter inside bladder) หรือ ectopic ureter
5. Renin–Angiotensin System (RAS) ใน Fetal Renal Development
5.1 Classic RAS Pathway
Angiotensinogen (liver) → Renin (kidney) → Angiotensin I → ACE → Angiotensin II → acts on AT1 + AT2 receptors
AT1 receptor: renal tubular growth + branching; trophoblast invasion in placenta; vasoconstriction uterine spiral arteries
AT2 receptor: acts as growth brake via apoptosis → prevents uncontrolled tubule growth; ↑ in fetal kidney vs adult
5.2 Sex-Specific RAS Programming
Testosterone → ↑ renin + ↑ AT1 receptor → upregulates vasopressor arm of RAS (Angiotensin II/AT1) → higher resting BP in males
Estrogens → upregulate vasodepressor arm (ACE2 + AT2) → lower resting BP in premenopausal females
Sex differences in CKD risk after prematurity may partly reflect these RAS programming differences (Moritz KM, Placenta 2010)
5.3 Antenatal Glucocorticoids and RAS
Short course antenatal steroids: ↓ GFR + ↑ insulin resistance ใน young adults who had prenatal glucocorticoid exposure (Chehade, Curr Pediatr Rev 2018)
Animal models: antenatal glucocorticoid exposure → altered DNA methylation + elevated blood pressure — epigenetic mechanism confirmed

⚠️ Antenatal Steroids: ประโยชน์ระยะสั้น vs ผลระยะยาวต่อ Kidney
Short course steroids ใน preterm birth = standard of care (ลด RDS, IVH, NEC). แต่มีหลักฐานว่า even 1 course → ↓ GFR + ↑ insulin resistance ในผู้ใหญ่. Repeated courses ยิ่งมีผลมากขึ้น. สรุป: ยังใช้เมื่อ indicated แต่ หลีกเลี่ยง repeat courses โดยไม่จำเป็น.
6. Renal Ascent
Kidney ascent: sacral region → first lumbar vertebra (L1); previously thought due to spinal straightening — recent studies suggest renal growth itself drives migration (Fukuoka, Anat Rec 2019)
Dense band: adrenal cortex + developing celiac ganglia + metanephros = solid band allowing simultaneous ascent of all 3 structures; dissipates after kidney reaches L1
Blood supply during ascent: metanephros is adjacent to umbilical artery (not segmental vessels as previously thought); final renal artery forms after kidney reaches L1 position
Renal hilum rotates 90°: ventral → medial orientation as ascent occurs
Abnormal ascent → ectopic kidney (pelvic/horseshoe); see Ch75
7. Fetal Programming — Prematurity, LBW, และ CKD
7.1 Brenner Hypothesis

Brenner hypothesis (Kidney Int 1996): Low nephron number → each nephron hyperfiltrates to meet excretory demands → single-nephron hypertension → glomerulosclerosis → nephron loss → systemic HTN → CKD. Cycle accelerates progressively.
Evidence: hypertensive individuals have 133% higher mean glomerular volume but 46.6% fewer glomeruli vs normotensive controls
IUGR: ~30–35% nephron deficit; may develop secondary glomerular HTN + nonnephrotic FSGS over time
7.2 Preterm Birth และ CKD Risk
Preterm birth incidence: up to 14% of live births worldwide; >95% survive to adulthood (>18 years)
Long-term mortality: 30–50% increased risk from early to mid-adulthood vs term-born individuals
Preterm + LBW → nephron deficit → CKD risk increases; even early term (37–38 wks) has significantly ↑ CKD risk
LBW (<2500 g): ↑ CKD risk regardless of GA; Crump, BMJ 2019: national cohort — prematurity = independent risk factor for CKD from childhood → mid-adulthood
7.3 Environmental Factors ที่ลด Nephron Number

rows: Nephrogenesis ceases ex utero; ↓ T3 environment
Lifelong nephron deficit; CKD + HTN risk
Placental insufficiency → ↓ nephron formation; IUGR → 30–35% deficit
↓ prostaglandin E2 → renal hypoperfusion → vasomotor nephropathy
Alters DNA methylation; ↓ GFR + ↑ insulin resistance long-term
Retinoids regulate nephron number + vascular supply
Low vitamin A → IUGR → ↓ nephron number
Tubular stretching → ↑ intracellular Ca²⁺ → apoptosis → tubular atrophy
Early relief ≠ full renal recovery if in utero

blocks.text: 7.4 Postnatal Nutrition — EUGR และ Kidney
EUGR (extrauterine growth restriction): ↓ postnatal growth + adverse long-term CV + renal outcomes
High-protein formulas: ↑ catch-up growth แต่อาจมี adverse renal and cardiac effects from protein overload on immature kidneys
RCT: breastfed preterm infants → lower BP + better insulin sensitivity at adolescence vs formula-fed preterm infants
8. Epigenetics and Renal Programming
Epigenetics = gene expression changes without changing DNA sequence: DNA methylation, histone modifications, noncoding RNAs
Preterm birth + postnatal NICU stressors → alter epigenetic imprinting → lifelong changes in renal gene expression
Antenatal glucocorticoids in animal models → ↑ DNA methylation of specific genes → elevated BP — concern for similar effects in human fetuses
Early studies: epigenetic alterations of angiogenic progenitor cells in preterm infants — affects kidney + cardiovascular systems simultaneously
Emerging target: identifying reversible epigenetic marks as therapeutic targets to improve long-term renal outcomes in preterm infants
9. NICU Practical — Renal Development ในชีวิตประจำวัน
Preterm infants < 34 wks: explain to family that kidney development ยังไม่สมบูรณ์ — GFR ต่ำ, tubular function อ่อน; monitor electrolytes carefully, avoid nephrotoxins
Prenatal NSAID exposure: assess for oligohydramnios, neonatal AKI, renal tubular dysfunction — risk highest during period of active nephrogenesis
Antenatal glucocorticoids: อธิบาย benefit vs long-term renal risk to parents; document number of courses; avoid unnecessary repeat courses
Renal programming counseling: preterm + LBW infants should have long-term nephrology follow-up: annual BP + urinalysis (proteinuria screening) throughout childhood and into adulthood
Dysplastic kidney (Ch75): understanding renal morphogenesis helps explain why RET/GDNF mutations cause bilateral agenesis vs ectopic ureter vs VUR — point of disruption determines phenotype
WT1 pathway: neonatal male with undescended testes + ambiguous genitalia + proteinuria → think Denys-Drash (WT1 mutation) — early nephrology referral, Wilms' tumor surveillance
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Preterm Birth = Nephron Deficit ตลอดชีวิต
Nephrogenesis สิ้นสุดที่ 34–36 wks — ทารกที่คลอดก่อนนี้ทุกราย ได้ nephron น้อยกว่าปกติ. >60% ของ nephrons สร้างใน T3. ดังนั้น VLBW + ELBW = highest risk for lifelong CKD + HTN. แนะนำ annual BP screening + urinalysis ตั้งแต่ childhood ไปจนถึง adulthood — อย่ารอให้มีอาการ.

PEARL 2 — RET–GDNF = Master Switch ของ Ureteral Bud
ถ้า GDNF signal ผิดตำแหน่ง (anterior/posterior to normal site) → ureteral bud evaginates ectopically → ectopic ureter, VUR, or UPJ obstruction. Delayed apoptosis ของ common nephric duct = UPJ obstruction; overactivation = VUR. เข้าใจ pathway นี้ = เข้าใจ CAKUT pathogenesis.

PEARL 3 — Brenner Hypothesis ใน Practice
LBW infant → fewer nephrons → each nephron hyperfiltrates → BP ค่อยๆ เพิ่ม + proteinuria develops ใน childhood/young adulthood. Counsel parents: LBW = not just a newborn problem — it's a lifelong renal risk factor. Brenner BM, Kidney Int 1996: paradigm shift ใน nephrology.

PEARL 4 — Antenatal Steroids: 1 Course = Long-Term Renal Impact
Short course betamethasone/dexamethasone = evidence-based for preterm benefit. แต่ Chehade 2018: แม้ 1 course → ↓ GFR + ↑ insulin resistance ในผู้ใหญ่. Animal models confirm: epigenetic methylation + ↑ BP. Message: ใช้เมื่อ truly indicated แต่ avoid repeating without clear benefit.

PEARL 5 — S-shaped Body = Nexus ของ Kidney + Vascular Development
S-shaped body stage: podocytes หลั่ง VEGF → endothelial progenitors migrate เข้า cleft → glomerular vasculogenesis เริ่ม. นี่คือเหตุผลที่ VEGF dosage ต้องพอดี: too little = absent glomeruli; too much = aberrant capillary loops. Glomerular filtration barrier = podocyte foot process + GBM + fenestrated endothelium.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Brenner BM et al. Kidney Int 1996;49:1774 (Brenner hypothesis): Low nephron number → glomerular hyperfiltration → hypertension → CKD. Paradigm shift framing LBW as lifelong renal risk.

Crump C et al. BMJ 2019;365:l1346: National cohort — preterm birth independently increases CKD risk from childhood into mid-adulthood; risk highest at extreme prematurity but present even at early term (37–38 wks).
Crump C. Early Hum Dev 2020;150:105187: Adult health outcomes after preterm birth: >95% survive to adulthood; 30–50% excess mortality early-to-mid adulthood; CKD, HTN, metabolic disease dominate.
Chehade H et al. Curr Pediatr Rev 2018;14:219: Preterm birth: long-term CV and renal consequences — ↓ GFR + ↑ insulin resistance even after 1 short antenatal glucocorticoid course.

Moritz KM et al. Placenta 2010;31(Suppl A):S40: Sex-specific programming: testosterone upregulates AT1 vasopressor arm; estrogens upregulate ACE2/AT2 vasodepressor arm — explains sex differences in BP + CKD risk.
Fukuoka K et al. Anat Rec 2019;302:278: Embryonic ascent of the kidney revisited — renal growth (not spinal straightening) drives migration; metanephros adjacent to umbilical artery during ascent.
Stewart K, Bouchard M. Semin Cell Dev Biol 2014;36:13: Coordinated cell behaviours in urogenital morphogenesis — RET–GDNF pathway in ureteral bud formation + apoptosis patterns.
Schell C et al. Semin Cell Dev Biol 2014;36:39: Glomerular development — shaping the multi-cellular filtration unit; podocyte VEGF drives vasculogenesis at S-shaped body stage.
Herzlinger D, Hurtado R. Semin Cell Dev Biol 2014;36:50: Patterning the renal vascular bed — podocyte VEGF secretion drives regional branching; VEGF also mediates vasa recta development postnatally.

Blake J, Rosenblum ND. Semin Cell Dev Biol 2014;36:2: Renal branching morphogenesis — RET–GDNF–Wnt11 positive feedback loop drives ureteral tip branching; inhibited by SPRY1 at stalk to prevent ectopic budding.
Little MH. Organogenesis 2011;7:229: Renal organogenesis: 37 anchor genes defined for 6 sub-compartments; each has absolute spatial specificity for differentiation program.

Bohnenpoll T, Kispert A. Semin Cell Dev Biol 2014;36:21: Ureter growth and differentiation — BMP4 signals distal ureteral mesenchymal specialization; Sonic hedgehog from epithelium stimulates smooth muscle formation.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: อธิบาย 3 kidney systems ในลำดับพัฒนาการ — pronephros, mesonephros, metanephros — fate ของแต่ละ system คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Pronephros: week 3–4, degenerates completely — rudimentary ใน humans. Mesonephros: week 4–8, transient filtration function; ♂ = ส่วนหนึ่งกลายเป็น vas deferens + epididymis; ♀ = degenerates. Metanephros: permanent kidney; differentiation เริ่ม ~week 5, nephrons functioning ~week 8; nephrogenesis สมบูรณ์ 34–36 weeks. Nephron count: 200,000–2,000,000 per kidney. >60% ของ nephrons สร้างใน trimester 3.
Q: RET–GDNF pathway ในการสร้าง ureteral bud: ถ้า GDNF signal อยู่ผิดตำแหน่ง จะเกิด anomaly อะไร?

ตอบ: GDNF (from metanephric mesenchyme) → activates RET (on nephric duct) → ureteral bud evagination. ถ้า GDNF signal อยู่ anterior/posterior to normal position → ureteral bud forms ectopically → ectopic ureter in bladder/urethra. Delayed apoptosis ของ common nephric duct → UPJ obstruction. Overactivation of apoptosis → vesicoureteral reflux. RET–Wnt11 loop drives continued branching — all branching occurs at ureteric tips only.
Q: S-shaped body stage สำคัญอย่างไรต่อ vasculogenesis? ผิดปกติจะเกิดอะไร?

ตอบ: ที่ S-shaped body: podocytes หลั่ง VEGF → endothelial progenitors migrate เข้า cleft → initial capillary loop → mature glomerular tuft (6–8 loops). นี่คือจุดที่ nephrogenesis กับ vasculogenesis เชื่อมกันครั้งแรก. ถ้า VEGF ไม่เพียงพอ → absent glomeruli หรือ hypoplastic glomerular tuft. ถ้า VEGF มากเกิน → aberrant capillary formation. Podocyte foot process integrity = critical ต่อ filtration barrier; loss → proteinuria.
Q: WAGR syndrome กับ Denys-Drash syndrome ต่างกันอย่างไร? WT1 role คืออะไร?

ตอบ: WT1: zinc finger transcription factor (chr 11p13); essential ต่อ podocyte development + GDNF expression. WAGR: deletion 11p13 (WT1 + PAX6) → Wilms' tumor + Aniridia + GU abnormalities + mental Retardation. Denys-Drash: WT1 point mutation (dominant negative) → mesangial sclerosis + Wilms' tumor + gonadal dysgenesis; severe early-onset nephrotic syndrome. Clinical tip: neonate ♂ + undescended testes + proteinuria → think Denys-Drash.
Q: Brenner hypothesis คืออะไร? clinical evidence อะไรสนับสนุน?

ตอบ: Brenner hypothesis (Kidney Int 1996): low nephron number → hyperfiltration per nephron → single-nephron HTN → glomerulosclerosis → nephron loss → systemic HTN → progressive CKD. Evidence: hypertensive individuals have 133% higher mean glomerular volume + 46.6% fewer glomeruli vs normotensive. IUGR = 30–35% nephron deficit. LBW → ↑ adult CKD + HTN risk (Crump BMJ 2019). Females เสี่ยง similar to males แม้จะมี ~12% fewer glomeruli baseline.
Q: Antenatal glucocorticoid exposure มีผลระยะยาวต่อ kidney อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Short course = standard of care; ลด RDS, IVH, NEC. แต่ Chehade 2018: even 1 short course → ↓ GFR + ↑ insulin resistance ในผู้ใหญ่. Mechanism: glucocorticoids → potent regulators of fetal growth → repeated doses → adverse metabolic, CV, renal consequences. Animal models confirm: antenatal steroids alter DNA methylation → ↑ BP (epigenetic imprinting). Clinical implication: ใช้เมื่อ truly indicated; หลีกเลี่ยง repeat courses โดยไม่จำเป็น.
Q: Perinatal obstructive uropathy มีผลต่อ nephron number อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Obstruction during/after nephrogenesis: tubular stretching → activates ion channels → ↑ intracellular Ca²⁺ → apoptosis → tubular atrophy + ↓ nephron number. Animal model (unilateral ureteral obstruction): after relief — tubular atrophy, ↓ glomerular number, ↓ GFR ใน obstructed kidney; contralateral = compensatory growth แต่ both kidneys show glomerular sclerosis + interstitial fibrosis. Obstruction ใน adults ไม่ทำให้ nephron ลดเหมือน fetal/perinatal obstruction — window of vulnerability คือ during active nephrogenesis.
Q: Sex differences ใน renal development และ CKD risk: เกิดจากอะไร?

ตอบ: (1) Females มี ~12% fewer glomeruli baseline แต่ CKD risk หลัง preterm birth similar to males. (2) RAS sex-specific programming: testosterone ↑ AT1 vasopressor arm; estrogens ↑ ACE2/AT2 vasodepressor arm → premenopausal females have lower resting BP. (3) Some studies: premature females มี ↑ CKD risk vs males; others: males 1.3× ↑ risk. (4) Female fetuses less vulnerable to adverse in utero environmental factors ใน some studies. (5) Epigenetic sex differences in RAS expression may also play role.
Day 75 Preview

Avery Chapter 75 — Developmental Abnormalities of the Kidneys
พรุ่งนี้: CAKUT (Congenital Anomalies of Kidney and Urinary Tract) — armed ด้วยความเข้าใจ renal morphogenesis จากวันนี้ เราจะเรียน: unilateral agenesis, bilateral agenesis (Potter syndrome), ectopic kidney, horseshoe kidney, MCDK, kidney dysplasia, ARPKD vs ADPKD, ciliopathies (Joubert, Bardet-Biedl, Jeune, Meckel-Gruber), Beckwith-Wiedemann, renal tubular dysgenesis, kidney teratogens — และ neonatal dialysis in NICU.
